finding: Slovak firms face persistent margin compression as input costs outpace selling prices
bullets: Slovak firms anticipated average selling price growth of 4.27% over the next 12 months (n=418), easing 0.36 pp from wave 37's 4.63%, yet non-labour input prices were expected to rise 8.31% (n=417) — a gap of 4.04 pp signalling continued margin compression.

Wage growth expectations for Slovak firms fell moderately to 4.21% (n=441), down 1.28 pp from 5.49% in the previous wave, but remained well above the EA average of 2.80%, compounding cost pressures alongside the elevated input price outlook.

Slovak firms' five-year inflation expectations rose 1.51 pp to 8.57% (n=163), the largest increase across all horizons and countries in wave 38, widening the gap above the EA's 6.28% and pointing to persistently higher domestic price expectations.
chart_subtitle: Slovak firms expect 8.31% input price rises vs 4.27% selling prices.
